Home / Research Projects / H-RAG for Cyber Threat Intelligence

H-RAG for Cyber Threat Intelligence

A hierarchical retrieval-augmented generation system for cyber threat intelligence analysis, designed to preserve document structure across executive summaries, technical details, and atomic evidence units.

RAGKnowledge GraphsCTILLM EvaluationHallucination Control
View code

Overview

Hierarchical RAG pipeline for analyst-grade cyber threat intelligence reasoning. Combines structure-preserving document segmentation, FAISS-based vector retrieval, a lightweight CTI knowledge graph, and local LLM inference, with citation-grounded response generation and explicit hallucination controls. The system is designed to separate evidence-grounded answers from speculative reasoning, and to make the chain of retrieved evidence inspectable.

Methods

FAISS IVF, Sentence-Transformers, hierarchical chunking, lightweight CTI knowledge graph, local LLM inference, citation-grounded generation.

Evaluation

Internal evaluation across factual accuracy, citation accuracy, retrieval performance, hallucination rate, and latency.

Key Contributions

  • Hierarchical document segmentation across executive, technical, and atomic evidence layers.
  • FAISS IVF vector retrieval combined with knowledge-graph traversal.
  • Local LLM inference with citation-grounded response generation.
  • Explicit evidence linking to reduce hallucination in CTI reasoning.
  • Designed for analyst-grade workflows rather than end-user chat.

Research & Engineering Challenges

  • Preserving structural information across heterogeneous CTI document formats.
  • Balancing retrieval recall with citation precision for evidence-grounded answers.
  • Designing evaluation that distinguishes hallucinations from defensible inference.

Tech Stack

Python FAISS IVF Sentence-Transformers Ollama / Local LLM Knowledge Graphs Hierarchical chunking RAG

Status

Accepted at international conference, 2026
